enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from his Boulder apartment on June 9, 2014, recounting a motorbike trail ride in the mountains where he attempted to cross a snowfield near Rollins Pass toward Winter Park but turned back. He reflects on mechanical concerns (loose spoke, rear tire wear), limited work done on his website, and weighs whether to ride his motorbike to work.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: riding with clutch engaged on steep climbs
stances.position: Dislikes it — considers it unsafe or improper technique
Skeptical — worried about tire wear and cost, leaning toward limiting it to when his friend Garrett rides with him
Enthusiastic — wants to know them by heart

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was in Boulder Vila on June 9th, 2014.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I waited for my water to boil and then poured it into oatmeal.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I put in my password yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to work yesterday and then came back.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went motorbike cycling in the mountains.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tried to go over Roll and pass to Winter Park but encountered a big snow field.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My front rail couldn't get over the snow field.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I could have ridden over the left part where the snow was less.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I turned around and saw guys in Jeeps.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: They wanted me to try going through another snow field or trail, possibly leading to Eldora.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I considered going up a really steep section but went too slow and had to put the clutch in.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't like going up steep sections with the clutch engaged because it's not a smooth way to ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I turned back because I couldn't turn around.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rode some cool evituary trails I hadn't been on before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought about learning the motorbike trails by the back of my hand.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I came home and realized I should adjust everything before riding again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My spokes are loose, and I should probably fix that.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not sure if I'll go riding today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I only did a little bit of work on my website last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I should probably work on my website today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My water just boiled.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm thinking about how big houses are today compared to one month ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might ride my motorbike to work today for the second time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was thinking about the rear tire getting chewed up quickly if I ride the motorbike more.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might go back to that plan and focus on the website.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder Vlog for, I guess, yesterday, I don't know what yesterday was, but for Monday, the 9th or something of June 2014.
I'm waiting for my water to boil, and then I pour the water in the oatmeal.
Yesterday, I, um, putting in my password here.

Yesterday, I, uh, went to work, and then I came back, and then I went motorbicling, motorbicycling, up in the mountains until I had, tried to go over Rollins Pass to, uh, uh, Winter Park, but then there was this big snow field.
I tried to drive right over it, but, um, like, my front reel, couldn't get the front reel over the snow.
I could have ridden over the left part where the snow was hard, but I decided to turn around.
I then saw these guys on Jeeps, and they wanted me to try to go through some other snow field and some other trail, which I think goes to Eldora.
I was like, nah, and I left.
Then I tried to go up this really steep thing, but I went up just a little bit too slow at one point without putting in the clutch, and it stalled.
I don't like going up these things with having the clutch engaged.
That's not, that's not the style-y way.
So then I, but I had to, I had to, like, push it backwards because I couldn't turn it around.
And then, uh, did some cool adventuring around trails I haven't been on.
Oh, man, I'm like, I could really learn this by the back of my hand on the motorbike.
But, uh, yeah, and then came home, and I should probably make sure everything's adjusted before I go out riding again.
And one of my spokes is loose, so maybe I should fix that, I don't know.
But I don't know if I'll go riding today.
I only did a little bit of work on my website last night, so I should probably work on that today.
Ooh, and there's my water.
It just got boiled, so I've got two houses, big houses a day, whatever.
It's been a long month, so I think I'm going to ride my motorbike to work today for the second time.
And, um, I was thinking about riding the motorbike later on.
The rear tire is going to get chewed up pretty quick, and I don't know, I don't really have money to spend on it, so.
My original intention was only riding it when, like, Aaron wants to go with me.
So maybe I should go back to that plan.
I know, mate.
Ah, that's so I can focus on the website.
Thinking out loud.
Okay, peace out.
Okay, peace out.
